title: magnolia candollei var. angatensis
intro: Magnolia candollei var. angatensis, also known as the Philippine magnolia, is a plant species within the Magnoliaceae family, native to the Philippines and parts of China. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. The health benefits of Magnolia candollei var. angatensis include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's bark and leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
